extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN won by one thousandth of a second over DENNIS in a 9.7km Jerusalem ITT, both clocking 12:04.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS set benchmark tempo of 48.1 km/h at halfway, revealing pacing strategy elite TTers must match to contend.
GONÇALVES exceeded expectations finishing 9 seconds down in 2nd place, suggesting Katusha scouting undervalued TT form indicators.
FROOME lost 35 seconds despite pre-race crash reconnaissance, exposing course familiarization risks when prioritizing marginal aerodynamic gains.
CAMPENAERTS matched DENNIS's 12:04 exactly, indicating identical power-to-weight output at equivalent aero positions on this flat course.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Won by 0.001 seconds over DENNIS, demonstrating superior pacing discipline to sustain 48+ km/h through finale.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: Set early benchmark at 48.1 km/h through halfway split, forcing all chasers into unsustainable early efforts.
rider_signals.GONÇALVES José: positive: Finished 2nd briefly at 9 seconds down as unexpected contender, revealing hidden Katusha TT depth.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 35 seconds after course-familiarization crash, suggesting high-risk recon strategy backfired on technical parcours.
rider_signals.CAMPENAERTS Victor: positive: Matched DENNIS at 12:04 exactly, confirming emerging elite TT status beyond hour-record specialist reputation.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: Set early reference time 33 seconds slower than DENNIS, establishing baseline for non-specialist strong TTers.
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Lost 48 seconds to DUMOULIN, exposing TT weakness that eliminates him from overall contention in balanced GTs.
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: Finished 25 seconds down, signaling decline from prime TT form that handicappers must adjust for.
